87.reason: [Error: This only clears the adjacency list of the deleted vertex, but does not remove edges from other vertices pointing to the deleted vertex. The problem requires deleting the vertex from the graph, meaning all edges incident to it should be removed. The reference program uses a different approach (marking visited), but here the graph structure is corrupted because other vertices still have pointers to the deleted vertex's adjacency list entries, which may cause undefined behavior or incorrect traversal.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This only clears the adjacency list of the deleted vertex, but does not remove edges from other vertices pointing to the deleted vertex. The problem requires deleting the vertex from the graph, meaning all edges incident to it should be removed. The reference program uses a different approach (marking visited), but here the graph structure is corrupted because other vertices still have pointers to the deleted vertex's adjacency list entries, which may cause undefined behavior or incorrect traversal.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: This only clears the adjacency list of the deleted vertex, but does not remove edges from other vertices that point to this vertex. The graph remains connected to the deleted vertex through other vertices' adjacency lists, which will cause traversal to still encounter the deleted vertex.]

41.reason: [Error: This only sets the adjacency list of the deleted vertex to NULL, but does not remove edges from other vertices that point to the deleted vertex. The graph still contains edges to the deleted vertex from other vertices, which will cause incorrect traversal results.]

122.reason: [Incorrect empty condition; should be front >= rear to correctly detect when queue is empty]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect empty condition; should be front >= rear to correctly detect when queue is empty]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect. The queue is empty when front == rear, not when front > rear. This can cause infinite loops or premature termination in BFS.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect; it should be front >= rear or front == rear to indicate empty queue. As written, it may cause underflow or infinite loop.]

111.reason: [The DFS traversal should always start from vertex 0 as per problem requirement, but this loop starts from the smallest unvisited vertex. Also, after deletion, the graph is still connected and traversal must start at 0.]
